stem: A rectangular prism has length $5 \text{ cm}$, width $3 \text{ cm}$ and height $2 \text{ cm}$. Find its surface area in $\text{cm}^2$.
hint: Use $SA = 2(lw + lh + wh)$. Calculate each pair of faces separately.

stem: A rectangular prism has length $6 \text{ cm}$, width $4 \text{ cm}$ and height $3 \text{ cm}$. Find its surface area in $\text{cm}^2$.
hint: A rectangular prism has 3 pairs of identical faces. Use $SA = 2(lw + lh + wh)$.

stem: A rectangular prism has length $8 \text{ cm}$, width $5 \text{ cm}$ and height $2 \text{ cm}$. Find its surface area in $\text{cm}^2$.
A rectangular prism has length $10 \text{ cm}$, width $3 \text{ cm}$ and height $4 \text{ cm}$. Find its surface area in $\text{cm}^2$.
hint: Identify each dimension, then apply $SA = 2(lw + lh + wh)$.

stem: A triangular prism has a right-angled triangular cross-section with legs $3 \text{ cm}$ and $4 \text{ cm}$. The length of the prism is $9 \text{ cm}$. Find the total surface area in $\text{cm}^2$.
hint: First find the hypotenuse using Pythagoras. Then: $SA = 2 \times \tfrac{1}{2}ab + L(a + b + c)$.
explanation: Hypotenuse: $\sqrt{3^2 + 4^2} = 5 \text{ cm}$. Two triangular faces: $2 \times \tfrac{1}{2} \times 3 \times 4 = 12 \text{ cm}^2$. Three rectangular faces: $9(3 + 4 + 5) = 9 \times 12 = 108 \text{ cm}^2$. Total: $12 + 108 = 120 \text{ cm}^2$.

stem: A triangular prism has a right-angled triangular cross-section with legs $5 \text{ cm}$ and $12 \text{ cm}$. The length of the prism is $8 \text{ cm}$. Find the total surface area in $\text{cm}^2$.
hint: The $5$-$12$-$13$ Pythagorean triple gives the hypotenuse. Find all five face areas and add them.
explanation: Hypotenuse: $\sqrt{5^2 + 12^2} = \sqrt{169} = 13 \text{ cm}$. Two triangular faces: $2 \times \tfrac{1}{2} \times 5 \times 12 = 60 \text{ cm}^2$. Three rectangular faces: $8(5 + 12 + 13) = 8 \times 30 = 240 \text{ cm}^2$. Total: $60 + 240 = 300 \text{ cm}^2$.

stem: A triangular prism has a right-angled triangular cross-section with legs $6 \text{ cm}$ and $8 \text{ cm}$. The length of the prism is $7 \text{ cm}$. Find the total surface area in $\text{cm}^2$.
hint: Recognise the $6$-$8$-$10$ triple. Add the areas of both triangular ends and all three rectangular sides.
explanation: Hypotenuse: $\sqrt{6^2 + 8^2} = \sqrt{100} = 10 \text{ cm}$. Two triangular faces: $2 \times \tfrac{1}{2} \times 6 \times 8 = 48 \text{ cm}^2$. Three rectangular faces: $7(6 + 8 + 10) = 7 \times 24 = 168 \text{ cm}^2$. Total: $48 + 168 = 216 \text{ cm}^2$.

stem: A triangular prism has a right-angled triangular cross-section with legs $9 \text{ cm}$ and $12 \text{ cm}$. The length of the prism is $5 \text{ cm}$. Find the total surface area in $\text{cm}^2$.
hint: The $9$-$12$-$15$ triple is a multiple of $3$-$4$-$5$. Calculate the two triangular ends and three rectangular faces.
explanation: Hypotenuse: $\sqrt{9^2 + 12^2} = \sqrt{225} = 15 \text{ cm}$. Two triangular faces: $2 \times \tfrac{1}{2} \times 9 \times 12 = 108 \text{ cm}^2$. Three rectangular faces: $5(9 + 12 + 15) = 5 \times 36 = 180 \text{ cm}^2$. Total: $108 + 180 = 288 \text{ cm}^2$.

stem: A rectangular storage box has no lid. It has length $50 \text{ cm}$, width $30 \text{ cm}$ and height $20 \text{ cm}$. Find the total surface area of the box (excluding the open top) in $\text{cm}^2$.
hint: An open-top box has 5 faces: one bottom and four sides. Do not include a top face.
explanation: Bottom: $50 \times 30 = 1500 \text{ cm}^2$. Two longer sides: $2 \times (50 \times 20) = 2000 \text{ cm}^2$. Two shorter sides: $2 \times (30 \times 20) = 1200 \text{ cm}^2$. Total: $1500 + 2000 + 1200 = 4700 \text{ cm}^2$.

stem: A rectangular gift box has length $25 \text{ cm}$, width $20 \text{ cm}$ and height $10 \text{ cm}$. How many $\text{cm}^2$ of wrapping paper are needed to cover the outside of the box exactly?
hint: The wrapping paper must cover all 6 faces. Use $SA = 2(lw + lh + wh)$.

stem: A wooden ramp is shaped like a triangular prism. Its cross-section is a right-angled triangle with base $60 \text{ cm}$ and perpendicular height $45 \text{ cm}$. The ramp is $80 \text{ cm}$ wide. Find the total surface area of the ramp in $\text{cm}^2$.
hint: Find the hypotenuse using Pythagoras. The surface has two triangular ends and three rectangular faces (base, slope, and back).
explanation: Hypotenuse: $\sqrt{60^2 + 45^2} = \sqrt{5625} = 75 \text{ cm}$. Two triangular ends: $2 \times \tfrac{1}{2} \times 60 \times 45 = 2700 \text{ cm}^2$. Three rectangular faces: $80 \times 60 + 80 \times 45 + 80 \times 75 = 4800 + 3600 + 6000 = 14400 \text{ cm}^2$. Total: $2700 + 14400 = 17100 \text{ cm}^2$.

stem: A rectangular room has length $4 \text{ m}$, width $3 \text{ m}$ and wall height $2.5 \text{ m}$. Tiles cost $\$35$ per $\text{m}^2$. The floor and all four walls are tiled. Find the total cost in dollars.
hint: Find the area of the floor plus all four walls. Then multiply by the cost per $\text{m}^2$.
explanation: Floor: $4 \times 3 = 12 \text{ m}^2$. Two longer walls: $2 \times (4 \times 2.5) = 20 \text{ m}^2$. Two shorter walls: $2 \times (3 \times 2.5) = 15 \text{ m}^2$. Total area: $12 + 20 + 15 = 47 \text{ m}^2$. Cost: $47 \times \$35 = \$1645$.
